blocks.content: Canis brought a teal robe.
Though Arin was watching, Akein changed without hesitation.
Leaning on the table, Akein shuffled to a chair and finally caught his breath.

blocks.speaker: Akein
blocks.content: Hah, I feel alive again.

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: Are you alright, Master?
His dark circles contrasted sharply with his pale skin, unmistakably marking him as a dark mage.

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: What splendid eyes. That's why I took him as my disciple.
In contrast, Akein wasn't fond of Arin.
Her black hair and eyes indicated talent, but she was weak-willed and lacked judgment.
Her pretty face showed no malice, and her large eyes betrayed every emotion.
Seeing the old man's naked body, her pupils trembled uncontrollably.
Akein clicked his tongue.

blocks.speaker: Akein
blocks.content: Tch, tch. What use is she? Utterly worthless.
If not for Canis's attachment, he wouldn't have brought such a fragile girl along.
Seven years ago, on that day.
Even with the life-support device, his mobility had deteriorated.
Summoning his last strength, he'd ventured into the capital, Vashka.
As the kingdom's largest city, its streets were wealthy and dazzling.
But behind the light lay the wretched lives of those cast aside by society.
The slum, Radum.
Reflecting the capital's extreme polarization, Radum's residents lived far more miserably than beggars elsewhere.
Their only means of survival was scavenging through trash bins—competition so fierce that murders over fish bones were commonplace.
There, Akein had found Canis and Arin.
In Radum, where daily life was war, ten-year-old Canis's survival tactic with Arin had been to grow cruel.
Canis's eyes burned with hostility toward the world.

blocks.speaker: Akein
blocks.content: My eyes didn't deceive me. He's the real deal.
Refusing Canis's help, Akein stood.

blocks.speaker: Akein
blocks.content: Today, of all days, I didn't want to show weakness—the day I'd escape wretched aging.

blocks.speaker: Akein
blocks.content: Witness the greatness of dark magic.
Akein clutched the black crystal and closed his eyes.
His hands shook weakly, but his face was alight with excitement.
Kuh—ah!
Gaaaaah!
Akein screamed like a monster.
Absorbing 40 years of power at once would've driven an ordinary person mad.
The concept of dark magic was absorption and assimilation.
Akein merged the dark power stored in the crystal with his own, reclaiming his former strength.
Uooooooh!
The shadow at his feet flared like flames, then fragmented and scattered as if breaking free.
Time passed.
Canis and Arin slowly opened their eyes.
No trace of Akein's frailty remained.
His appearance hadn't rejuvenated, nor had his back straightened.
But his complexion glowed, and his eyes brimmed with vitality.

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: Feeling an aura of invincibility from his restored master, Canis trembled with awe.

blocks.speaker: Akein
blocks.content: Is this Master's true form? I'd heard rumors, but this is astounding.

blocks.speaker: Akein
blocks.content: Let's go. From today, Akein's history will be rewritten.
After waiting 40 years for this moment, Akein left the dungeon without savoring the surge of power.
Akein arrived at Inferno, the western prison of Vashka.
Housing criminals from class D to B, it boasted impregnable security.

blocks.speaker: Guard
blocks.content: Halt! Who goes there? This is a restricted area!

blocks.speaker: Guard
blocks.content: Identify yourself! Failure to comply will result in arrest!

blocks.speaker: Akein
blocks.content: Greenhorns…
Akein's shadow stretched, merging with the guards'.
BOOM
An explosion rocked the building.

blocks.speaker: Guard
blocks.content: Contact the palace! Request reinforcements! It's a mage!
The guards charged with weapons drawn.
But numbers meant nothing to a dark mage.
Akein's shadow extended like tendrils, merging with theirs.

blocks.speaker: Guard
blocks.content: Guh—what is this?!

blocks.speaker: Guard
blocks.content: I can't move!
The guards were paralyzed.
Then, with a wave of Akein's hand, they turned on each other, stabbing against their will.

blocks.speaker: Arin
blocks.content: Agh! Are you insane? This is dangerous!

blocks.speaker: Guard
blocks.content: I don't know! It's not me!

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: He must have a plan. Let's follow.
Akein strolled through the battlefield toward the building.
When the shadows connecting him to the guards snapped, the brawling men foamed at the mouth and collapsed.
This was the hallmark of dark magic—mind control.
Darkness merged with other darkness. By infiltrating another's shadow, one could control their body and thoughts.
On paper, dark magic was overpowered.
Yet it remained marginalized because one flaw overshadowed all its strengths.
It was weak to light.
Under sunlight, darkness existed only as slivers of shadows.
The stronger the light, the more mental energy was needed to cast dark magic.
While dark mages reached their peak at night, even that advantage waned with the popularization of photon theory, pioneered by the genius Kergos.
With dark magic students nearly extinct across the continent's magic academies, its decline in magical society was evident.

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: Yes, I must trust Master. He gave me everything.
Breaking through the entrance, Akein dispatched more guards inside.
He then entered Block A-3, where prisoners were held.
Sensing the commotion, inmates banged their meal trays against the bars.

blocks.speaker: Inmate
blocks.content: Hey! Don't care who you are—get us out!

blocks.speaker: Inmate
blocks.content: Open this damn gate! We'll make it worth your while! Or we'll kill someone for you!
Akein slowly raised his hands.
The prison's grim atmosphere was the perfect stage for his prowess.

blocks.speaker: Akein
blocks.content: Authority of Darkness.
Shadows on the floor morphed into hands, rising three-dimensionally.
Hundreds of hands gripped the bars, wrenching them apart with metallic screeches.
Bang! Bang! Bang! Bang! Bang!
The Authority of Darkness manipulated shadows to absorb surrounding energy, wielding them like limbs.
A single shadow could barely lift a pebble, but hundreds working in concert could rend iron bars with monstrous strength.
Once the gates were open, the inmates staggered out, heavy iron balls chained to their ankles.

blocks.speaker: Inmate
blocks.content: What, you a mage? Damn impressive.

blocks.speaker: Inmate
blocks.content: Thanks anyway. Name's Crown, the Night Cat. Folks in this hellhole know me.
Akein scowled at their wretched aura.
Forty years ago, Inferno had been different.
Once filled with the worst of the worst, it had fallen into disrepute.

blocks.speaker: Akein
blocks.content: Pathetic. Criminals introducing themselves? You think you're leaving just because the bars are gone?

blocks.speaker: Inmate
blocks.content: The hell, old man? Lost your marbles? We were trying to be nice!
The inmates burst into laughter.

blocks.speaker: Inmate
blocks.content: Pfft—ha! What's this geezer babbling about? You think we'll just go back after this?

blocks.speaker: Inmate
blocks.content: Let's crush him! I'm sick of this damn prison!
Drunk on freedom, they charged recklessly.
But they soon realized their ankles were still shackled.

blocks.speaker: Inmate
blocks.content: Huh? What the—damn it!

blocks.speaker: Inmate
blocks.content: Panic set in.
Akein watched their pitiful display and snorted.
To choose evil, one needed strength. Lacking that, at least intelligence. These were just parasites, wasting food in prison.

blocks.speaker: Akein
blocks.content: Authority of Darkness.
The hands that had torn the bars now wrapped around the inmates, dragging them into the darkness.
Mmph! Ugh!
Grrrrk!
Muffled screams were all they could manage.

blocks.speaker: Arin
blocks.content: Canis, is this necessary for revenge?

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: Arin, denying Master is denying ourselves.

blocks.speaker: Arin
blocks.content: But still…

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: Have you forgotten Radum? Worse things happened there. These prisoners are no different from those who tormented us.

blocks.speaker: Arin
blocks.content: But we promised. To forget everything from Radum. To be happy from now on.

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: Nothing's over yet. Until Master's vengeance is fulfilled, we won't find happiness.
As the darkness faded, the inmates lay twisted on the ground, bones shattered.
Akein looked down at them disdainfully.

blocks.speaker: Akein
blocks.content: Weaklings. Was this a waste of time? Or are the better ones in another block?
Clank. Clank.
Just then, the sound of chains came from a cell at the end of the hall.
A wolf-like man stepped forward, and Akein's eyes gleamed with interest.

blocks.speaker: Man
blocks.content: Heh, unbelievable. Do you know where this is? Inferno, the western prison. Planning treason?

blocks.speaker: Akein
blocks.content: Treason. A delightful notion. Care to join?

blocks.speaker: Man
blocks.content: Tch, so strict.

blocks.speaker: Man
blocks.content: If you're breaking me out, why not?
The man scratched his head with his cuffed hands.
As his eyes glinted between his arms, he lunged without warning.
Crunch!
The man grimaced as he landed.

blocks.speaker: Man
blocks.content: Tch, annoying magic.
Akein hadn't budged.
The side of his face struck by the iron ball was covered in black shadow.
Dark Skin, a spell maximizing dark magic's absorption to nullify impact.

blocks.speaker: Akein
blocks.content: Not bad. You pass.
Akein's Authority of Darkness summoned shadows that seized the man's shackles and cuffs.
Don't move. Unless you want to lose limbs.
Before the man could retort, the shadows sharpened into blades and spun.
The chains snapped instantly, leaving his skin unharmed.
Limitless shaping and precision were dark magic's pinnacle.

blocks.speaker: Akein
blocks.content: Enjoy your freedom. Follow me. You have a role to play.

blocks.speaker: Man
blocks.content: What's this? I never agreed to join.

blocks.speaker: Man
blocks.content: Money moves you, doesn't it? Help me, and you'll be rich.

blocks.speaker: Akein
blocks.content: Heh, 'rich' better not be chump change.
Akein left the prison with his new follower in tow.
Shirone's group rented an inn after organizing their research from the library.
Two days of discussion had outlined the photon cannon's principles.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Why does mass exist?

blocks.speaker: Neid
blocks.content: Without mass, nothing would change. And that wouldn't be so bad. Repeating the same day wasn't the end of the world.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Mass isn't weight. Weight is force exerted in gravity's direction. But space has no direction. So what is mass? Cohesion? But that's not a perfect explanation either.

blocks.speaker: Neid
blocks.content: Then what do you think it is?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Existence.

blocks.speaker: Neid
blocks.content: Huh?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Proof of being in this space. That's mass.

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: Hmm. Sounds plausible. Like the Creator's will or breath.

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: The God Particle.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: What?

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: The God Particle. Not bad, right? Let's call the particle Shirone discovered the God Particle.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: I love it. And this is important.

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: Right. A mage's power is tied to feeling. Using a name that resonates is best.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: In that sense, the God Particle was an excellent choice.

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: Alright, let's wrap up the analysis and head back to school tomorrow. Classes resume the day after, so mentally prepare yourselves. Don't tell me you've forgotten our promise already?

blocks.speaker: Neid
blocks.content: Of course. Moving up to Class Four this semester. From now on, we're studying like mad. Worst case, Shirone might graduate before us.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Hah! What are you talking about? Right now, I'm the most likely to break our promise. At least you two are top-tier.

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: Can't sleep. I'll study a bit before bed.

blocks.speaker: Neid
blocks.content: Huh? Then me too!

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: I've met good friends.
The Royal Education Academic Council.

The Royal Academic Conference, hosted by the council, brought together magic and swordsmanship schools, monastic orders, academic factions, alchemy foundations, and the elite 99 Artists to discuss the kingdom's future.

blocks.speaker: Sade
blocks.content: Ugh, damn it. Don't these people sleep? Let me go home.
Drafted by lottery, he was miserable.
A former delinquent, he had no interest in education, voluntary or otherwise.

blocks.speaker: Sade
blocks.content: The only silver lining was the number of attractive female teachers. Especially the woman across the round table—his ideal type.

blocks.speaker: Sade
blocks.content: Her wavy blue hair and playful demeanor, even in formal wear, were irresistible.

blocks.speaker: Sade
blocks.content: She's adorable. Ozente Reina, was it?

blocks.speaker: Sade
blocks.content: A member of the 99 Artists, the kingdom's premier artistic group, she attended as a pianist under the mentorship of Melhen, the Melodious.
